text: I have a headache. You should rest.
Can I have a painkiller, please?
Excuse me, how do I get to the station?
Hello. I have a headache and a fever. Can I have a painkiller, please?
Of course. You should rest at home. Read the label first.
Thank you. How much is it?
It is twelve reais. Get well soon!
Excuse me, how do I get to Pinheiros station?
Go straight and turn left. It is near.
How do I get to the station?

Hello. Let me tell you about a normal day. In the morning, I did not feel well. I had a headache and a fever. So I went to the doctor. The doctor said: You should rest and drink water. Then I went to the pharmacy. I said: Can I have a painkiller, please? The pharmacist asked: Do you have a prescription? I said: No, just a painkiller. I asked: How much is it? It was twelve reais. On the way home, I was lost. So I asked a man on the street: Excuse me, how do I get to Pinheiros station? He said: Go straight and turn left. Now I am home. I rested, and I feel much better.

Hello. Let me give you three simple tips for real life in English. Tip one: at the doctor, say how you feel. I have a headache. I have a fever. The doctor will say: You should rest. Tip two: at the pharmacy, ask politely. Can I have a painkiller, please? And to know the price, ask: How much is it? Tip three: when you are lost, ask for help. Excuse me, how do I get to the station? People will help you. Go straight, turn left, turn right. You can do all of this now. Be calm, speak slowly, and take your time.

First, I tell the doctor: I have a headache. Second, the doctor says: You should rest. Third, at the pharmacy I ask: Can I have a painkiller, please? Fourth, I ask: How much is it? Fifth, on the street I ask: Excuse me, how do I get to the station?
Can I have a painkiller, please?
How do I get to the station?
